methods.description: Process for creating a large set of self-consistent assigned experimental transitions with uncertainties, and the associated inverted experimentally-derived energy levels with uncertainties.

steps.searchable_summary: conduct a comprehensive literature review on spectroscopic data
outputs: list of relevant spectroscopic data sources

steps.description: Digitisation and consolidation of all available assigned transitions into a single format.
steps.searchable_summary: digitize and consolidate spectroscopic transitions into a unified format

steps.description: Using the MARVEL software program, process data within first individual sources then multiple sources to produce a self-consistent spectroscopic network by increasing uncertainties and removing transitions as required to ensure self-consistency of the whole spectroscopic network.
steps.searchable_summary: process data with MARVEL software to ensure self-consistency of spectroscopic network

methods.description: Process for approximating the energy of quantum states in a molecule based on each state's quantum numbers using model Hamiltonian parameters.

steps.description: Obtain reasonable functional forms for the dependency of diatomic energy levels on molecular quantum numbers by approximating the full Hamiltonian of the system.
steps.searchable_summary: approximate Hamiltonian to model diatomic energy levels

steps.description: Adjust parameters to maximize the fit between the predicted and experimental transition frequencies.
steps.searchable_summary: fit model parameters to experimental transition frequencies

methods.description: Process for producing data entirely from ab initio quantum chemistry electronic structure calculations to enable the calculation of energies of rovibronic quantum states in a diatomic molecule.

steps.description: Perform multi-reference configuration interaction (MRCI) calculations based on orbitals obtained from state-selective or state-averaged Complete Active Space Self-Consistent Field (CASSCF) calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform MRCI calculations using CASSCF orbitals

steps.description: For the lowest lying electronic state of a given spin-symmetry with little multi-reference character, use CCSD(T) with basis set extrapolation.
steps.searchable_summary: use CCSD(T) with basis set extrapolation for low-lying electronic states
